description: Value used after ownership has been transferred (moved)
rationale: Accessing moved-from memory is undefined behavior; Redox enforces move semantics at compile time
fix_template: Clone the value before the move: `v x2 = x.clone(); move(x); use(x2)`
alternatives: Restructure code to avoid the move
Use a reference instead of taking ownership

rationale: Each value has exactly one owner; moving transfers ownership, making the source invalid
fix_template: Clone before the first move: `v x2 = x.clone()`

description: Value is cloned but the original is not used afterward
rationale: Unnecessary clones waste memory and CPU cycles; move semantics can be used instead
fix_template: Remove the clone and move the value directly

description: Struct partially moved; remaining fields accessed
rationale: Moving a field out of a struct makes the entire struct invalid unless remaining fields are individually accessed
fix_template: Move the entire struct, or destructure it first
alternatives: Use Option<T> for the moveable field

description: Value moved inside a loop body; unavailable in subsequent iterations
rationale: Loop iterations reuse the same variable binding; moving in one iteration leaves it invalid for the next
fix_template: Clone inside the loop or restructure to produce a new value each iteration
alternatives: Move the value out of the loop

description: Implicit Copy of a large value type (>64 bytes); consider using a reference
rationale: Large Copy types cause expensive memcpy on every assignment; references are O(1)

description: Function returns a value that has already been moved
rationale: Cannot return ownership of a value that was transferred elsewhere
fix_template: Return before moving, or clone the value

description: Value boxed (^T) but could remain on the stack
rationale: Heap allocation has overhead; stack-allocated values are faster for small types
fix_template: Remove the Box and use the value directly
alternatives: Keep Box if the value needs dynamic dispatch

description: Arc used where Rc would suffice (single-threaded context)
rationale: Arc has atomic reference counting overhead; Rc is lighter in single-threaded code
context: no Send/Sync bounds on containing scope
alternatives: Keep Arc if the code may become multi-threaded later

description: Value dropped while outstanding borrows exist
rationale: Dropping a value while references to it exist creates dangling pointers
fix_template: Extend the lifetime of the value or reduce the borrow scope
alternatives: Clone the value before creating the borrow
